source_pr.title: log-backup: implement subscription of flush
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: Ref https://github.com/tikv/tikv/issues/13824

Problem Summary:
In the post, we were polling the checkpoint of each region from TiKV and calculating the global checkpoint, which isn't so real-timed. 

### What is changed and how it works?
This PR adapts the new interface which supports subscribing flush events.
Generally, we added a new actor named `FlushSubscriber` to 

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
Tested via:
- TPCC workload, happy path.
- TPCC workload, restart cluster every 30 mins.
- TPCC workload, with one node using older version TiKV (which doesn't support subscribing)
- TPCC workload, upgrading from older version.


### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
Improve the RPO of PITR.
```

symptoms: No explicit flaky failure signature is described in the PR or reflected in the patch; the changes implement log-backup flush subscription and refactor BR streamhelper checkpoint/advancer logic.
failure_signature: No explicit flaky failure signature in patch (feature/refactor).
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR introduces log-backup flush subscription and refactors BR streamhelper components. While it touches concurrency-related code, the patch does not provide a concrete flaky failure signature or a targeted stabilization fix for an intermittent failure, so keep this case as needs-more-evidence.
fix_pattern: Implement log-backup flush subscription and refactor streamhelper checkpoint/advancer logic; no targeted flaky stabilization evidence in patch.
